coredata.dc:description: Neurodegenerative diseases are heterogenous group of diseases arises in the diverse segments of brain such as hippocampus, cerebellum, and brain stem due to protein accumulation, gene mutations, synaptic loss, mitochondrial dysfunction in neuronal cell. Neuronal disorders including Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), and Prion's disease, caused by combination with multiple factors. Contrast reagents are proficient to access the damaged cells or tissues to crossing blood brain barrier (BBB). All these creates troublesome for the contrasting agents for detection of impairments in the brain. BBB is limits entry of threpaeutic agent in brain there evolved a field in the nanotechnology known as nanotheranostics where both diagnostic and therapeutic effects were achieved. The semiconductor nanocrystals called quantum dots (QDs) are the efficient drug delivery systems accomplishing the objective of theranostic activity. The quantum dots are the zero-dimensional nanostructures with particle size of 2–10 nm, intermediary between bulk semiconductors and discrete molecules owing outstanding optical and electrochemical properties due to their characteristics like luminescence, photostability, electronic properties, high excitation capacity, size tunable emission etc. These features preserve their applicability in the diagnostics, bioimaging, biosensing and therapeutics etc. The QDs show their efficiency in diagnosing and treating the neurodegenerative diseases as they are capable of crossing BBB and biocompatible with the neuronal cells with low cytotoxicity. This article provides the knowledge of quantum dots, their preparation processes and their theranostic activity over the neurodegenerative diseases.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Neurodegenerative diseases are heterogenous group of diseases arises in the diverse segments of brain such as hippocampus, cerebellum, and brain stem due to protein accumulation, gene mutations, synaptic loss, mitochondrial dysfunction in neuronal cell. Neuronal disorders including Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), and Prion's disease, caused by combination with multiple factors. Contrast reagents are proficient to access the damaged cells or tissues to crossing blood brain barrier (BBB). All these creates troublesome for the contrasting agents for detection of impairments in the brain. BBB is limits entry of threpaeutic agent in brain there evolved a field in the nanotechnology known as nanotheranostics where both diagnostic and therapeutic effects were achieved. The semiconductor nanocrystals called quantum dots (QDs) are the efficient drug delivery systems accomplishing the objective of theranostic activity. The quantum dots are the zero-dimensional nanostructures with particle size of 2–10 nm, intermediary between bulk semiconductors and discrete molecules owing outstanding optical and electrochemical properties due to their characteristics like luminescence, photostability, electronic properties, high excitation capacity, size tunable emission etc. These features preserve their applicability in the diagnostics, bioimaging, biosensing and therapeutics etc. The QDs show their efficiency in diagnosing and treating the neurodegenerative diseases as they are capable of crossing BBB and biocompatible with the neuronal cells with low cytotoxicity. This article provides the knowledge of quantum dots, their preparation processes and their theranostic activity over the neurodegenerative diseases.

ce:section.ce:para: Nanotechnology refers to the miniaturization of the particles at the nanometer size range (1–100 nm) with desirable structure and properties. The advancement of nanotechnology in the medical and pharmaceutical field had progressed extensively, including the usage of nanoengineered devices that act directly at target sites interacting with the biological systems at molecular levels for treating various diseases as well as lowering the adverse effects [1,2]. Alternatively, this nanocarriers are recently explored as theranostics, dual role of therapeutic and diagnosis, to achieve the precise treatment when the disease is diagnosed [1,3,4]. Application of nanotheranostics with target specific diagnosis and therapy of the disease is greatly necessary for neurodegenerative diseases (NDs) [3,5]. Amongst the nanoengineered drug delivery systems, quantum dots (QDs) are the emerging technology that has the potential of theranostic application which is detected in systematic process of nanolithographic studies in 1980 b y Alexie Ekimov [6,7]. These QDs are artificial semiconductor nanomaterials with tiny particle sizes and 3-dimensional confinement of excitons. They have incredible, fluorescent properties, high photostability, excitation capacity, size-dependent emission, and electrochemical features, which makes them applicable for drug delivery, targeting, diagnosis, bioimaging, photo-radiation treatment (PDT), and sensing for various diseases [6,7].

Application of QDs in biomedical and pharmaceutical fields shows the importance in pharmacokinetics studies within the human body system. The absolute information regarding ADME of QDs is still lacking [7]. The QDs are administered widely through the parenteral route whereas intradermal and inhalation routes are also suitable to target specific treatment sites, where the QDs are absorbed through receptor-mediated endocytosis process at the cellular level and may get accumulated in the targeted tissues [7–9]. The distribution of QDs occurs through blood as they are administered parenterally, whereas the metabolism is a critical process as the core of the structure contains toxic substances. The shell and any coatings of QDs can be metabolized by photolysis and oxidation reactions, thus degradation products formed may shield the core substances to release [10]. The QDs are excreted by interacting with the plasma proteins [11].

NDs are considered to be complex disorders of the central nervous system because of the unavailability of proper detection and diagnosis technique [12]. For the purpose of diagnosing, NDs with contrast agent requires to cross the brain-brain barrier (BBB) to reach the site of disease for imaging, however, delivering a theranostic agents across the BBB is a great challenge for imaging and treatment into the central nervous system [ [2,13–15]]. The basal layer of endothelial cells surrounds the blood vessels, which are covered by the layer of astrocytes connected to the neuron. These endothelial cells contain fenestrae and endocytic vesicles unlike other blood capillaries in various organs [16,17]. Finally, the structural integrity of the BBB is evidenced by the layer of pericytes, which are bound together compactly with the junctions between the brain cells without any gaps [18]. The endothelial cells forms base of brain through basal lamina in which the laminin, type IV collagen, fibronectin, heparin sulphate are structural material [19]. Thus, the BBB creates a rigid barrier by blocking 98% of the drugs reaching the site of action that can treat neurological disorders [20–22]. Recently, the challenge of delivering therapeutics or diagnostic agents to the central nervous system has been explored by the exploration of nanomaterials, where the neuro-nanomedicine with engineered nanostructures is designed to treat NDs by interacting at discrete atomic stages [23–25]. These nanoengineered devices have the ability to act, react, interlink with tissues for showing required action along with reducing adverse effects [26]. They were designed to increase the permeation through BBB, to target special cells, and to locate the exact target cells [21] They are being efficient carrier techniques for treating a variety of NDs as they could permeate to BBB in required dose of drugs with their biosensing applications [27]. QDs are one of the explored nanostructures for the treatment and diagnosis of disorders in the nervous system due to their nanosized architecture by which they can permeate through the rigid BBB [28]. In this review, special emphasis on the emerging nanomaterial, QDs, has been made with special emphasis on synthesis, and the applicability in different NDs, such as Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD) and Prion's disease.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Quantum dots as an emerging nanocarrier
ce:section.ce:para: QDs are intermediate between bulk semiconductors and discrete molecules or atoms having a higher surface area to volume ratio which results in excePLional electrical properties [29]. Due to their special configuration, their properties lie between the bulk semiconductors and molecules. Inorganic core and the outer shell part provides of semiconductor, oPLical behaviors to QDs, and for passage of active core part respectively. Larger QDs emit red light due to their smaller energy band gaps whereas smaller QDs emit blue light due to their larger energy band gaps and this is used for nanosensing [29]. These semiconductor nanocrystals are having 2–10 nm particle size, which has vital oPLical properties such as light absorPLion, emission, etc as their primitive charcterstic [29]. QDs could be generated using elements incuding Group II-VI (CdTe, ZnS, and CdSe), III-V (InAs and InP), and IV-VI (CS, CSe, PbS and PbSe) from the chemical table. They contain semi-conductance material as the core material, which contains further encapsulation of other higher energy bandgap semiconducting substances which enhances ocular features in QDs and deduce chemical interaction. The shell on the QDs is an essential part acts as a barrier, QDs are zero-dimensional colloidal nanostructures in which the electrons are confined or restricted to move in all three directions length, breadth, and height, making the electronic energy levels of atoms or molecules individually distinct [7,29,30].

The energy levels in semiconductors have a highly occupied valence band with fully emPLy conduction band that splits them by certain energy. Due to the thermal or light absorPLion capacity that exceeds the energy of the bandgap, the excitation of electron occurs from the valence band to the conduction band via hole in the valence band. The excited electron acts as a charge carrier or interacts with the hole due to electrostatic attraction. This electron-hole pair is known as exciton and the distance between them is known as exciton Bohr radius. The reduction of size to few nanometers confines the exciton Bohr radius than actual, thus it is said to be a quantum confinement effect. The Bohr radius is a characteristic feature of different semiconductor materials. This confinement effect is a unique feature of QDs, which makes the discrete energy levels from the band leading to excellent electronic properties. While the return of excited electron to valence band leads to emission of light or photon provides beneficial oPLical properties to the QDs [30]. The larger band gaps and intrinsic dipole moments with zero dimensions of QDs lead to excellent electronic properties [29]. QDs possess size-tunable luminescence emission because of their quantum confinement effect and transmission of wavelengths from visible to infrared wavelength in the electromagnetic radiation (EMR) spectrum. Thus, these QDs can produce different colors depending on the size of the particles, small QDs have high energy bandgaps and emit blue light (low wavelength) whereas large QDs have lower energy bandgaps and emit red light (high wavelength) [29].

The QDs are generally classified into three types. The first type, simple/core type QDs formed with a single component using chalcogenides (sulfide, telluride, and selenide) of zinc, cadmium, lead, etc (PbS, PbSe, CdS, CdSe, CdTe, etc.), where the photoelectric properties of the in these QDs can be easily tunable [31]. The carbon core type QDs are of two types, graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and carbon quantum dots (CQDs). They combinedly form a new class of semiconductor nano-crystals having an excessive attraction for their highly tunable fluorescence property and are obtained as significant, exceptional, and universal fluorophores. Depending on their characteristics, they can be polymeric dots prepared by the monomer or polymers and can be interacted with semiconductor nanoparticles like Ag3PO4, TiO2, and Fe2O3 [32–35]. The second type are core-shell QDs are consisting of a core with a small bandgap, which is encapsulated by a large bandgap semiconductor substance to improve quantum yield by passivating groups in nonradiative mode, where the properties can be changed effectively. For example, the CdSe core is coated with a ZnS shell to enhance quantum yield [31]. The third type, alloyed QDs with different bandgap consisting of two semiconductors material are joined together along with internal textures to form alloyed particles, but size cannot be tuned. For example, CdSeS/ZnS alloyed QDs emits various wavelengths of light at 6 nm diameter shown in (Fig. 1 ) [31].

Among various QDs, CQDs are extensively emerging and being the effective competitors to traditional QDs due to characteristics like better biocompatibility, lower toxicity, easy fabrication, economic effectivity with great oPLical and fluorescence properties which gained major interest in recent years [36]. The CQDs has various applications in treatment of NDS. For example, the Na-citrate derived CQDs has capability of suppression of amyloidogenesis of prion-like protein in Hen-egg white lysozyme (HEWL) model by preventing fibrillation from intermediates and solubilizing the both monomeric and oligomeric HEWL by various mechanisms which also showed very low cytotoxicity [37]. Koppel et al. reported that CQDs as efficient therapeutic agent for the pathogenesis of AD caused by gut microbiome which produced the bacterial endotoxins like lipopolysaccharide (LPS). The aggregation of amyloid plaques increased by LPS and production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) was mitigated by inhibitory effect of CQDs in zebrafish model [38].

The QDs can deliver both small and large drug moieties across the BBB via carrier-mediated transport using proteins and ligands like Glucose (GLUT1), Glutamate (ACST2) due to its features like extreme small size and adaPLable surface ability [23]. Also, the QDs can penetrate the neuron cells by the process of endocytosis, an energy-dependent and natural entry pathway for the extracellular substances. Endocytosis of nanomaterials into the cells is undergone by two mechanisms called phagocytosis and pinocytosis. Where macromolecules are uptaken by phagocytosis through specific cellular systems (neutrophils, macrophages, monocytes, etc.) and small particles were endocytosed by pinocytosis [39]. Receptor-mediated endocytosis is another type of endocytosis method in which plasma membrane forms inward budding to form vesicles with proteins for internalization and cross the BBB (Fig. 2 ). The QDs can easily permeate by the above-mentioned process in a time-dependent manner with a low risk of cytotoxicity. It does not cause any effects on cell viability, cell proliferation, cell differentiation and cellular metabolic activities. Thus QDs are considered as very good biocompatible nanostructures with neuronal tissues [40]. To further proceed, the synthesis processes of the QDs are summarised in the connecting section of the article.

This QDs synthesis is mainly used to prepare semiconductor nanocrystals and for large-scale purposes, where the colloidal chemistry is followed by reaction of precursor to form products dispersed in suitable organic solvents. The stability of dispersed QDs is obtained by heating the precursor solution at higher temperatures to degrade its structure and interact completely to form desired products thus, the temperature and injection time are crucial parameters for stabilization of the preferred composition [41]. Alternatively, stabilization can be achieved by maintaining the appropriate annealing prerequisites along with the monomeric concentration for the crystallization to form the monodispersed formulation. Hence, the monomeric concentration is a critical parameter, which regulates the size and provides smaller particles with narrow size distribution at high concentrations and large particles with broad size distribution at low concentrations. QDs of different kinds containing binary and ternary compounds could be synthesized by this method [42].

The hydrothermal process or hydrothermal carbonization is the simple single-stage method, economic, eco-friendly, non-hazardous, commonly used method of synthesizing QDs using a variety of organic precursors [43,44]. The semiconducting nanocrystals are prepared by an oxidation reaction with aqueous medium under high temperature induces pressure in Teflon layered hydrothermal reactor. By this heating, the appropriate vapor pressure is generated resulting in the crystalline QDs with less or no surface flaws [42]. In this method, temperature is an essential parameter in the formation of QDs with good texture. Small highly arranged crystalline QDs are formed at lower temperatures and poorly arranged QDs are formed at a higher temperature. The QDs with various size can be formed and has tunable fluorescence [6]. QDs synthesized by the hydrothermal process show narrow size distribution which is observed from the absorPLion spectrum [42].

In this process, QDs are fabricated by chemical reactions in organic phase (e.g., benzene, dimethylformamide (DMF), dimethyl sulfoxide, etc.) with the application of temperature and pressure under acidic or basic conditions [6]. This process is particularly used for producing crystalline substances. This improves the solubility of materials by decreasing viscosity or dielectric constant by heating the organic phase [42]. The size of produced QDs is dependent on the organic solvent chosen [6]. By this process, the chalcogenides, metal oxides, and sulfides are synthesized by laboratory scale and their particle size and morphology can be regulated by oPLimum circumstances of pressure, temperature, pH, additives, time, and concentration. With this single-step process, the nanomaterials of required constituents, phases, and framework can be produced [41]. The solvothermal synthesis is a well-accepted method of synthesizing QDs as it produces QDs of narrow size distribution and higher quantum yield [42,45].

This method is also known as microwave irradiation method. It is a fast and cost-effective method of preparing QDs [35,43,46] and provides a higher quantum yield [6]. These QDs are produced from reacting precursors with convenient organic solvent under microwave radiation for about less than 1 min [35,43,46,47]. The formed QDs have greenish-yellow fluorescence and are in harmonized size due to the homogeneity of temperature provided. The reaction time of this process is an important parameter, which moderates the size of QDs accordingly [6]. This process is mainly applicable for reactions containing carbohydrates as precursor, and the CQDs could be synthesized by taking sucrose as a source and diethylene glycol (DEG) as a reaction medium for stabilization. These DEG carbon dots formed were reported to be stable and there were no appreciable peaks observed indicating no shift in photoluminisence (PL) over a range of 320 nm–380 nm excitation. Furthermore, they could be dispersed well in water and transparent at appearance, have lesser cellular toxicity signifying their applicability in bioimaging and screening [35,43]. The intensively fluorescent CQDs were formed by pyrolysis reaction between citric acid and different amine molecules, in which the primary amine molecules act as surface passivating agents and N-doping originators thus, enhancing the PL [48]. The quantum yield of these CQDs is reliant on the amount of nitrogen and showed great compatibility and applicability in the biomedical field [35,43]. This process has become popular due to the associated advantages, rapidity, short reaction time, homogenous heating, improved yields, and purity of the final substances.

Under tough and critical preconditions, the smaller organic substances are carbonized to carbonaceous products by stronger oxidizing agents, which are then cut into smaller layers by restricted oxidation reaction [49–52]. The luminous CQDs can be prepared in the aqueous phase by the simple method described by Peng and Travas-Sejdic, which includes the dehydration of carbohydrates with concentrated H2SO4 and HNO3. These products could be condensed into discrete CQDs and adsorbed surface with amine-terminated substances for obtaining PL proprties. The different starting material and the extent of exposure to nitric acid can regulate and tune the emission wavelength of these CQDs.

The nontoxicity and polychrome properties of CQDs increase their applicability to life sciences research [53]. Shen et al. produced PL CQDs through HNO3 oxidation by one-pot synthesis by sourcing a cationic branched polyelectrolyte (like polyethyleneimine (PEI)), functions as a source and passivating agent [49]. The luminescence of these CQDs was greatly dependent on pH, unlike the generally prepared pH-sensitive CQDs. The PL characterized CQDs prepared by one-pot synthesis is inversely proportional to pH i.e., the PL intensity decreases with an increase in pH, hence this mechanism makes them essential for cellular metabolic processes by releasing protons. These are used in HeLa cell imaging because these are lesser cytotoxic, compatible and easily get penetrated HeLa cells [35,43].

In this technique, the nanocarbon materials are used as precursors with organic solvents, where they could be suspended. The glass cell containing sonicated suspension was radiated by Nd:YAG pulsed laser of second harmonic wavelength at 532 nm, in which QDs are obtained in the supernatant after centrifugation [54]. Alternatively, CQDs are prepared by laser ablation method with carbon starting material under water vapor, with argon as carrier gas at 900 °C and 75 kPa [55,56]. If refluxed in HNO3 for 12 h, the formed carbonaceous materials were cleaved into nanometric CQDs. Fabricating the surface in the passivation step with small organic substances like bis(3-aminopropyl)-terminated polyethylene glycol (PEG1500N) and poly (propionylethyleneimine-coethyleneimine) (PPEI-EI), 4,7,10-trioxa-1,13-tridecanediamine (TTDDA), oleylamine, with acid-treated CQDs were investigated, which were reported to provide bright fluorescence emission [57]. Hu et al. described the laser irradiation method for the formation of fluorescent CQDs from the non-aqueous solution of carbon, where the light emission is adjustable by changing surface conditions using different organic solvents [35,43,56].

This is an effective method of producing CQDs from large or small carbonaceous substances using small molecular weight alcohols. The QDs are prepared using two PL sheets as the working and auxiliary electrode, and a calomel electrode as a reference electrode fixed on a movable Luggin capillary. Under alkaline conditions, the CQDs are formed from the alcohols immediately after preparation [58]. The obtained QDs can be capable of increasing size and graphitization with enhancing applied potential. In addition, the QDs had amorphous core with tremendous excitation and size-tunable luminescence features without any passivation [35,43]. The differences in method of fabricating QDs have thier pros and cons which are summarised in Table 1
, whereas in Fig. 3 all the different methods of fabrication is summarised (seeTable 2).

Functionalization or surface modification is an effective technique for tuning the features of the substances for desirable applications. The functionalization of the QDs can be done utilizing surface chemistry, where covalent bonding, coordination bonding, p–p interactions, and sol–gel technology could be incorporated [59–61]. Covalent bonding is the most useful functionalization method for oxygen and amine-rich groups owing to their feasibility. To enhance PL characterstics of QDs, amine groups are linked on the surface via covalent bonding of particles. The modification of fluorescent CQDs by hydrothermal synthesis of 2Na.EDTA (3 nm size) with bluegreen fluroscence emission. The spiropyrans emits flurossenc at 650 nm instead of 510 nm due to the transfer of energy among CQDs and spiropyrans after irradiation with ultraviolet rays [62]. The functionalized QDs reported being advantageous in terms of photo-reversibility and higher stability, and can be reversed by using visible light [43].

Coordination is an easy method for the detection of phosphate by emerging the off-on fluorescence probe using europium-adjusted CQDs in a complex matrix (e.g., artificial wetlands system) [60]. The detection is due to carboxyl groups of CQDs were coordinated with Eu3+ by fluorescence [43]. Sol-gel method is an effective process of modification of the surface with different functional groups. The amorphous QDs could be prepared using organo-silane as a coordinating solvent and passivated by methoxysilyl groups. The synthesized into pure QD films or monoliths transforms hydrophobic particles into hydrophilic with good bioavailability and less toxicity [43,63].

Consequently, doping is the method of introducing functional groups or impurities for tuning the PLproperties of QDs. There are a variety of doping methods in which different elements, like N, S and P, could be incorporated to improve the properties. The N-CQDs are the mostly doped QDs as they show N-dependent PLand two-photon conversion properties by increasing the emission in Fermi level and electrons in the conduction band [64]. The CQDs showed good photoluminescense, higher catalytic activity and tolerance to methanol crossover effects caused by heteroatom functionalization by doping with both P and N by microwave-assisted method of DMF in presence of phosphoric acid [43]. Such functionalized and doped QDs are widely explored in the medical field. We have specifically focussed on the application of QDs in NDs. The connection section would emphasize the application of QDs in the diagnostic, treatment, or management of NDs.

The brain is major and the most complicated organ in the body consists of mainly two types of cells i.e., the neuronal cells and glial cells in which the neurons are responsible for critical and functions of brain. The brain is separated from the circulatory system through BBB and is safeguarded by the cranium [65]. The discrepancy in the functioning of neurons and interferences between the neuronal cells or any part of the brain leads to neuronal cell death, which results in conditions for NDs [66]. The word neurodegeneration means the damage of neuronal cells or deterioration of the brain [67,68].

Neurodegenerative disorders are a heterogeneous group of diseases associated with selective or progressive loss of neurons, cognitive impairment, difficulty in body movement, loss of speech and thinking abilities, anti-social behaviour, etc. [69,70]. Neurodegenerative diseases like AD, PD, prion's disease were characterized by specific loss of neuronal cells and formation of protein aggregates [71,72]. Neurodegenerative diseases can be initiated from various parts of the brain such as the hippocampus, cerebellum, brainstem. It is a complex disease as a coincidence of one or more medical conditions [68]. There are multiple affects that trigger a variety of neurodegenerative disorders at different parts of the central nervous system like protein accumulation, gene mutations, synaptic loss, mitochondrial dysfunction, or neuronal cell death, etc. [73].

AD is an age-linked condition mainly caused by dementia which is very predominant ND [72–74]. It is illustrated by gradual loss of neurons, cognition, function, decision making, judgment, and behaviour remarkably due to alteration in parts of the frontal cortex and hippocampus of the brain [75]. Memory impairment is an important syptom as the shortfall of neurons mainly occurs specifically at memory domains which leads to episodic dementia [76]. The foremost cause of AD is excessive production of amyloid-beta proteins and their incapability to eliminate from the brain leading to self-accumulation forming toxic plaques, oligomers, fibrils [72,77] and the hyperphosphorylation of tau proteins leading to accumulation of neurofibrillary tangles [78], thus causing synaptic damage and programmed cell death [72,79].

ce:section.ce:section-title: QDs in the treatment of AD
ce:section.ce:para: The Aβ pePLide is a polypePLide with 39–42 amino acids, in which Aβ1-40 and Aβ1-42 are the major variants in humans [80]. The abundancy and the fibril forming ability of Aβ1-40 and Aβ1-42 lead to the formation of Aβ peptides thus resulting in aggregation of peptides to form Aβ plaques. This aggregation of Aβ peptides, mature fibrils and soluble oligomers are toxic to neurons causing AD [81]. There are only a few approved drugs available in the management of AD's, however, the application of the treatments is limited to associated toxicities and the development of resistance [72,74]. Various peptides, organic molecules, peptide mimetics had shown promising results in AD, through the removal of aggregates or interfering with the formation of accumulates during the preclinical studies. However, they face challenges of low penetrability through BBB, lower in vivo stability, less efficacy and complicated synthesis that terminate their purpose for AD therapy [82]. GQDs, are efficient nowadays for the treatment of AD as they have the ability to supress the formation of amyloid plaques and also prevent the cytotoxicity caused by Aβ oligomers because of their ability to cross BBB (smaller size of 2–10 nm) and low cytotoxic nature. The hydrophobic interactions between carbon materials and Aβ1-42 peptides have the capability of amyloid plaques inhibition [83], which results in the lowering of negative surface potential, and this aids in enhancing the inhibiting activity of QDs [84].

There was a synergistic inhibitory effect of tramiprosate coupled covalently with GQDs observed against the Aβ aggregation in AD [85]. The GQDs conjugated with endogenous neuroprotective peptide glycine-proline-glutamate (Gly-Pro-Glu, GPE) to form GQDG nanomaterial which showed the inhibition of aggregation of Aβ1-42 fibrils in APP/PS1 transgenic mice when administered intravenously. The GQDG can cross Madin-Darby canine kidney (MDCK) cell monolayer by mechanism transcytosis because of smaller size and large specific surface area, specifically bind to the hydrophobic group of Aβ1-42 protein which enhances their inhibition, also improved the memory and learning capability of the mice providing AD treatment [86].

Similarly, Clitoria ternatea graphene QDs (ctGQDs) were synthesized by one-pot microwave-assisted green synthesis process by Tak and team, where they targeted the flowers of Clitoria ternatea for AD therapy. It had shown good stability, whereas memory learning and cognitive function radial arm maze and water morris maze assay showed reduced transfer latency to the hidden platform. The ctGQDs can cross BBB due to their smaller size, which demonstrated enhanced inhibition of acetylcholinesterase enzyme, reduced levels of lipid peroxide, nitric oxide, and enhanced level of glutathione protein. These favourable changes of the biochemical parameters indicated higher preservation, thus, considered to be effective in treatment of AD [87]. Alternatively, the cadmium-selenide/zinc-sulfide (CdSe@ZnS) QDs conjugated with the amino-polyethylene glycol (PEG) with negatively charged siRNAs adsorbed on the surface to form QD-PEG/siRNA nanoplexes. These fluorescent QDs are used to deliver siRNAs at targeting site. From Fig. 4 , it could be easily differentiated the green fluorescence at 488 nm was for siRNA, whereas the red fluorescence at 605 nm was for QDs. Following incubation for 45 min, there was fluorescence at the inner side of the cell membrane as mentioned orange in the merged figure Fig. 4a. Upon longer incubation, the QDs loaded with siRNA were moved and retained within the cytoplasm after 1.5 h (Fig. 4b). However, after 7 h of incubation, there was complete separation of green and red fluorescence, where the red levels were found more near to the cell membrane, whereas, the green levels were found to be more within the entire cytoplasm (Fig. 4c). The β-secretase (BACE1), which prominently enhances the transfection efficiency of siRNA, dispersed well in the cell bodies and suppresses the BACE1 gene resulting in the depletion of Aβ. The QDs are protected from high exposure to the intracellular environment and controlled the toxic Cd2+ released by biodegradable PEG polymer coating. Hence, the QD-PEG/siRNA nanoplexes were used for the delivering siRNA into neurons for suppression of AD associated BACE1 gene [88].

ce:section.ce:section-title: QDs in the diagnosis of AD
ce:section.ce:para: The peculiar features of QDs can overthrow the constraints of traditional dyes and imaging tools. QDs have the inherent capability to diagnose AD at early stage and can trace the in vivo states of the Aβ aggregation in mice. The fluorescent QD probes are conjugated with an anti-Aβ antibody, which are administered through intra-cerebroventricular route to healthy mice and transgenic mice containing human APP695swe and APP717 V–F mutations. The fluorescent of Aβ1–42 was observed regions including hippocampus, cerebral cortex, sagittal septum and striatum of APP transgenic mice as depicted in fluorescence microscopy, and in vivo imaging showed the reduced fluorescence intensity in contrast with the APP transgenic mice [89].

Similarly, CdSe@ZnS QDs were also explored as a biosensing carrier for the electrochemical detection of the ApoE-significant biomarker in AD. The evaluation of ApoE was done using immune-complex assay which was performed in a chip with flow mode. The tosyl-activated magnetic beads platform includes polydimethylsiloxane-polycarbonate microfluidic chip with integrated screen printed electrodes, indicating its importance as a biomarker in AD. CdSe@ZnS QDs showed a narrow range limit of detection with higher accuracy for diluted human plasma by square wave anodic stripping voltammetry [90]. The same research group had fabricated the CdSe@ZnS QDs for the detection of apolipoprotein E (ApoE). The sandwich immunocomplex microarray was tested with fluorescent dye Alexa 647 for comparative studies. The experiment was performed under similar conditions, where, for comparison, the conventional enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) targeting ApoE was used. The excitation wavelength results exhibited that QDs are highly efficient biosensing reporters in microarray assay with a lower limit of detection, five times and seven times more sensitive than Alexa microarray and ELISA, respectively [91].

QDs were also used as eminent fluorescent labels and had been applied for the detection of Aβ1-42 for sensitive and specific sandwich immunoassay. In this assay, streptavidin-QDs were conjugated to biotinylated anti-beta amyloid 1–16 (N-Ab). This N-Ab has the ability to identify target C-terminal antibody (C-Ab) and form sandwich, like C-Ab-Aβ1–42-N-Ab immunocomplexes in the presence of Aβ1–42. Thus, the concentration of Aβ1–42 was detected by the fluorescence intensity of the supernatant which makes it a prominent and rapid method. These also profitably applicable for the detection of Aβ1–42 in the human cerebrospinal fluid [92].
Overall, it is clear that the QDs are effectively explored in the treatment and diagnosis of Ads, where the results are quite promising to move forward.

It is a progressive degeneration of dopaminergic neurons which leads to the limited production of dopamine in the ventral trigeminal area and substantia nigra [68] resulting in movement control and other symptoms [93]. The habitual signs of PD are motor, mixed-motor and non-mixed types in which the most prevalent signs are tremors, bradykinesia, muscular rigidity, autonomic impairment, gait problems, cognitive impairment [ [68,94]]. There are other psychological symptoms like sleep disorders, depression, cognitive dysfunction etc. and some gastrointestinal symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and constipation [95]. Based on the seriousness of the disease the PD has been classified into mild, moderate and advanced types [68]. The etiology of PD is not clear to date but trusted to be including aggregation of intraneuronal intercytoplasmic inclusions of misfolded α-syn proteins in the neuronal cells known as Lewy bodies leading to the deterioration of dopaminergic neurons in substantia nigra and striatum of basal ganglia [94,96].

ce:section.ce:section-title: QDs in the treatment of PD
ce:section.ce:para: The PD is characterized by the accumulation and transmission of protein α-syn aggregates in the substantia nigra of the brain and no successful anti-aggregation agents were developed to date. The QDs show their effect by inhibiting the formation of Lewy bodies and fibrils. The mechanism underlying this is the dissociation of α-syn bodies into very small fragments of about 1 μm–235 nm and 1 μm to 70 nm range after kept for 6–24 h, respectively by binding between the negatively charged carboxylic group of QDs and positively charged region of Lewy bodies [97]. The number of fragments decreases as the time of incubation is increased in a time-dependent manner. The (α-syn preformed fibrils) α-syn PFF-induced pathogenesis seen in primary neurons can be prevented by QDs. They also prevent synaptic loss, neuronal cell death, decrease Lewy bodies and their production, ameliorate mitochondrial impairments, prevent transmission of α-syn preformed fibrils [98]. They rescue the death of dopamine secretory neurons stimulated by α-syn preformed fibrils, Lewy neurite pathology and behavioral effects [97]. GQDs migitates PD by inhibiting the fibrillization of α-syn along with solubilizing the mature amyloid fibrils into monomers [99].

In this disease, the shortage of dopaminergic neurons results in the deficiency of dopamine concentration in the brain leading to diffeerent complications. l-dopa, the precursor of dopamine provides efficient therapy for this disease by crossing BBB and then converting it into dopamine using l-amino acid decarboxylase or DOPA decarboxylase. But l-dopa shows adverse effects as the dose increases. Sheril et al. synthesized the chitosan carbon dots and encapsulated the dopamine in the matrix carrier to cross BBB and deliver the drug to effectively increase the level of dopamine in the brain without causing any side effects to the users [100].

Autophagy is a primitive biological process that decomposes the impaired cells, organelles and divergent proteins which is essential for the body. The pathogenesis of NDs includes abnormalities in the regular autophagy process, which require pharmacological compounds that induce the autophagy for neuroprotection particularly for PD models. Thus, nanoparticles are able to modulate the autophagic process and can be harnessed for NDs. Therefore, CdTe/CdS/ZnS QDs of core-shell-shell (CSS) type nanostructures were synthesized and their neuroprotective effects were analysed in pheochromocytoma PC12 cells from 1-methyl-4-phenylpyridinium ion (MPP+) induced cell damage. The MPP+ has been reported to inhibit the neuronal cells mitochondrial functions, lead to the sudden increase in ROS levels which results in the accumulation of the α-syn proteins. The CSS QDs induced autophagy in the cells by enhancing the Belcin-1 protein levels and suppressed the AKT/mTOR (protein kinase B/mechanistic target of rapamycin) signaling pathway. This downregulation of AKT/mTOR increases autophagy, aids in the clearance of the α-syn proteins. Enhanced clearance of α-syn mitigates the MPP+-induced neurotoxicity which was observed in increased viability and reduced cellular apoptosis [101]. The schematic representation of the neuroprotective action by the fabricated CSS QDs was represented in Fig. 5 , which revealed that the accumulation of α-Syn proteins was due to increased ROS levels and MPP+ inhibited mitochondrial function. Further, autophagy was promoted by the increase of Belcin1 protein with simultaneous AKT/mTOR downregulation [101].

GQDs were reported to be potential agents in the treatment of AD by inhibition of the formation of Aβ peptides fibrils. Likewise, GQDs function as anti-aggregation agents that prevented the fibrillation of α-syn. They interact with the mature fibrils directly and dissociated them into monomers. Their in vivo results exhibited a reduction in neurodegenration, synaptic proteins, reduction in the Lewy bodies and Lewy neurite formation. The α-synculien fibrils induce alleviation of mitochondrial impairments and sequential subcellular damages. The blockage of α-synucleinopathy transmission passage through BBB protection against dopaminergic neuron loss induced by exogenous α-syn preformed fibrils (PFFs) [102].

GQDs had shown the ability to inhibit formation of α-syn aggregates in PD through various mechanisms and interactions with monomers and fibrils. These aggregates were characterized by different studies including thioflavin-T fluorescence assays, dynamic light scattering, kinetic analysis, atomic force microscopy, circular dichroism, and fluorescence spectroscopy. The kinetic nature and efficiency of the inhibitory effect of GQDs had been shown that the GQDs interact with both monomers or mature fibrils through mechanisms such as adsorbing to aggregated species, altering the morphology, disassembling the interfilament interaction. Thus reduced aggregation rate of α-syn fibrils showed to prevent the formation leading to their greater potential in molecular events of amyloid fibrils [103].

ce:section.ce:section-title: QDs in the diagnosis of PD
ce:section.ce:para: Dopamine is a catecholamine neurotransmitter of the central nervous system, essential for various behavioral activities and functions of the body. The abnormal levels of dopamine may lead to several complications, NDs at lower dopamine levels whereas the higher levels increase the loss of energy through metabolism. In a report, l-cysteine capped Mn-doped ZnS QDs (L-cys ZnS:Mn QDs) were fabricated with a feature of superficial room temperature phosphorescence sensor for detection of DA levels in the brain. These reported results demonstrated orange emission band at ~598 nm by the QDs, which was reduced due to excess dopamine addition in alkaline medium. Thus, their results showed that QDs based sensors were highly selective and reproducible in detecting dopamine even at lower levels with very narrow detection limits. Hence, it could be inferred that these QDs are highly applicable for detecting dopamine in urine samples, biological fluids without interfering biomolecules such as amino acids, glucose, chloride, ascorbic acids etc. [104].

Ankireddy and coworkers developed a cysteine-capped indium phosphide/zinc sulphide QDs by modifying the surface of QDs with l-cysteine amino acid. It functions on coupling reaction for enhanced selectivity of dopamine for its detection. These fluorescent QDs undergo quenching with different concentrations of dopamine in presence of ascorbic acid resulted in the better selectivity of dopamine detection with a negligible limit of detection [105]. Alternatively, dopamine can be detected in the presence of tyrosine by a system containing the screen-printed electrode modified by GQDs (GQD/SPE). Different electrochemical methods like cyclic voltammetry (CV), chronoamperometry (CHA), and differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) techniques were used to evaluate the detection efficiency of the fluorescent electrode for dopamine and tyrosine. The separation oxidation peak potentials for dopamine and tyrosine were found at 435 mV with detection limits of 0.05 and 0.5 μM, respectively and were confirmed by different analytical methods [106].

Thioglycolic acid-capped cadmium sulfide QDs (TGA-CdS QDs) fluorescent sensor was fabricated to increase the sensitivity and selectivity for detection of dopamine. The dopamine was detected by the addition of 1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl) carbodiimide (EDC) and N-hydroxysuccinimide (NHS). It initiates the reaction between the carboxylic acid group of thioglycolic acid and the amino group of dopamine for formation of amide leading to quenching the fluorescence of the QDs. This sensor system was based on fluorescence of TGA-CdS QDs for detection of the dopamine with low sensitivity mixtures [107].

Nanocrystalline CQDs (C-dots) were synthesized by controllable chronoamperometry for the detection of dopamine and undergone various studies like UV–Vis, Raman, FTIR, fluorescence spectroscopy, atomic force and high-resolution transmission electron microscopy. The carbon dots were fabricated by electrochemical synthesis using 1-propanol as a carbon source and depending on the electrolysis time spent for the process and are applied on the surface of a glassy carbon electrode, resulting in the outstanding electrochemical response in the determination of dopamine and epinephrine with detection limits of 4.6 nmol L−1 and 6.1 nmol L−1, respectively. Thus, the electrode showed excellent performance with a better analytical response than single-wall carbon nanotubes and graphene oxide [108].

The fluorescence performance of GQDs had been improved by a brilliant pathway called element doping to modify the surface defective structures. Different element doped GQDs like N-doped, S-doped and S, N co-doped GQDs were prepared to evaluate the fluorescence sensing of dopamine in biomolecules. The N-doped GQDs, prepared by using urea as nitrogen source, were much effective owing to higher quantum yield (QY) of 78% and sensitive fluorescence quenching to dopamine than other GQDs. On the other hand, N-doped GQDs with ethylenediamine as nitrogen source had the highest quantum yield up to 95% with no quenching performance to dopamine. Thus, the N-doped GQDs with dual linear relation with the quenching intensity with dopamine had detection limits of 3.3 and 611 nM, respectively [109]. In summary, we could say that the QDs are a potential platform for analysis and detection of PD effectively with narrow detection limits.

Spongiform encephalopathy (SE), the other name of prion's disease, is defined by sponge-like progression of brain disease [110]. The prion's disease is a type of ND in which the deterioration of tissues occurs in the form of cysts or vacuoles making the brain a sponge-like structure by replacing its healthy tissues [68,111]. The pathogenesis of prion's disease lies in the aggregation of misfolded prion proteins mainly in the cerebrum cortex and cerebellum. Prions are the infectious proteins present in the cell membrane of certain neurons, consisting of about 253 amino acids in the form of three α-helix and two β-pleated sheets. The functions of prion proteins (PrPC) are unknown but it takes a part in the uptake of copper from the neuronal cells. These prions in mammals have the ability to reproduce with the help of normal protein leading to the conversion into its disease-causing isoform (PrPSc) [68,112]. The limited proteolysis of these misfolded proteins led to the formation of small, protease-resistant molecule of 142 amino acids, which polymerizes into amyloid with two α-helix and more β-strands [113]. These disease-causing isoform prion proteins have great affinity towards brain function, hence get accumulated in the neuronal cells and induce the apoptosis process causing the death of the cells [114]. Thus, the brain has a spongy form as the death of cells occurs numerous due to the formation of cysts and there is the formation of huge plaques due to the aggregation of prion proteins on the membrane of neurons which is harmful to the brain [68]. The symPLoms of prion disease include dementia, sleeplessness, paraplegia, ataxia, paresthesias, and deviant behaviour. But the major clinical manifestation of SE is Creutzfeldt–Jakob disease (CJD) due to the genetic mutations of PRNP gene [113].

ce:section.ce:section-title: QDs in the treatment of Prion's disease
ce:section.ce:para: The amyloidogenic determinant of the prion's protein that led to transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (TSEs) is 21-residue 106–126 fragment of PrP (PrP (106–126)) and membranes have a significant part in the formation of fibrillar plaques [115]. The chiral carbon dots prepared using l-lysine and d-lysine were used as the fibrillation modulators for PrP (106–126), showed the proficient impairment in the accumulation of β-sheet structures within PrP (106–126) accumulates. The atomic force microscopical and the cryogenic transmission electron microscopical representations are also supporting the same (Fig. 6 ). While the L-Lys-C-dots had demonstrated efficient effect in interruption of assembly of β-sheet of PrP and reduction in the major fibrils, prions plaques and high hydrophobic interaction compared to the D-Lys-C-dots [116].

ce:section.ce:section-title: QDs in the diagnosis of Prion's disease
ce:section.ce:para: In a current report, a photoelectrochemical (PEC) immunosensor with 3-mercaptopropionic acid (MPA)-capped CdTe QDs was used as photocathodic materials. This material was used to support the immobilization of antibody (Ab1). It was developed for the detection of prion proteins. This 3-Mercaptopropionic (MPA)-capped CdTe consisted of electrode surface incorporated with the bioconjugates of secondary antibody (Ab2)-gold nanoparticles-glucose oxidase (Ab2-AuNPs-GOx) for prion and its antibodies (Ab1 and Ab2) interactions, in the presence of prion protein. The PEC properties of MPA-CdTe QDs can be deteriorated by the addition of glucose which leads to GOx catalyzed oxidation of glucose and reduction of dissolved oxygen to reduce the photocurrent of the PEC platform, to attain optimal conditions where the PEC immune-sensor had shown the sensitive detection of prion protein with limit of detection of 0.73 pg mL−1 [117]. Thus, the QDs can be applied with specific modification for the treatment and assay of proteins in pharmacological treatment and biological analysis and diagnosis of Prion's disease, respectively.

QDs are semiconductor nanocrystals, possessing exclusive and excellent properties, like fluorescence, higher photostability and electrochemical properties. These properties are due to their zero-dimensional structure, where they are confined through the quantum confinement effect between bulk and discrete molecules. They posses tunable fluorescence properties due to their size-dependent characteristics for large QDs gives red emission due to small energy gap and small QDs give blue emission due to larger energy gaps. They are fabricated by different methods like solvothermal process, hydrothermal process, colloidal synthesis, etc., and can be functionalized or doped and to improve their properties. They are highly applicable in the biomedical and pharmaceutical fields, for drug targeting, diagnosis and imaging of the cells. They are used in the diagnosis and therapy of NDs because of their lower particle size, high permeability. QDs are biocompatible nanostructures with negligible cytotoxicity, cell proliferation, cell differentiation, and cellular metabolic activities. Their PL properties provided the pathway to develop biosensing techniques and bioimaging applications for detecting biomolecules, diagnosis of diseases, cellular and tissue imaging, neurological biomarkers, and in-vivo imaging for early diagnosis of disease going to be a major application for NDs.
